---
title: eda.plots
---



`eda.plots`



## Functions

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [plot_ip_boxplots](#spotoptim.eda.plots.plot_ip_boxplots) | Generate infill-point boxplots (ip-boxplots). A separate ip-boxplot is generated for each numerical column in a DataFrame, arranged in a grid. |
| [plot_ip_histograms](#spotoptim.eda.plots.plot_ip_histograms) | Generate infill-point histograms (ip-histograms) for each numerical column in the DataFrame within a single figure. |

### plot_ip_boxplots { #spotoptim.eda.plots.plot_ip_boxplots }

```python
eda.plots.plot_ip_boxplots(
    df,
    category_column_name=None,
    num_cols=2,
    figwidth=10,
    box_width=0.2,
    both_names=True,
    height_per_subplot=2.0,
    add_points=None,
    add_points_col=['red'],
)
```

Generate infill-point boxplots (ip-boxplots). A separate ip-boxplot is generated for each numerical column in a DataFrame, arranged in a grid.
Each subplot has its own scale, similar to how histograms are shown in plot_histograms().
Additional points can be added and highlighted in red.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name                 | Type                                           | Description                                                                     | Default    |
|----------------------|------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| df                   | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | DataFrame containing the data to plot.                                          | _required_ |
| category_column_name | [str](`str`)                                   | Column name for categorical grouping. Defaults to None.                         | `None`     |
| num_cols             | [int](`int`)                                   | Number of columns in the subplot grid. Defaults to 2.                           | `2`        |
| figwidth             | [int](`int`)                                   | Width of the entire figure. Defaults to 10.                                     | `10`       |
| box_width            | [float](`float`)                               | Width of the boxplots. Defaults to 0.2.                                         | `0.2`      |
| both_names           | [bool](`bool`)                                 | Whether to show both variable names and categories in titles. Defaults to True. | `True`     |
| height_per_subplot   | [float](`float`)                               | Height per subplot row. Defaults to 2.0.                                        | `2.0`      |
| add_points           | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | DataFrame containing additional points to highlight. Defaults to None.          | `None`     |
| add_points_col       | [list](`list`)                                 | List of colors for the additional points. Defaults to ["red"].                  | `['red']`  |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description   |
|--------|--------|---------------|
|        | None   | None          |

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}


::: {#1a81efba .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import pandas as pd
from spotoptim.eda.plots import plot_ip_boxplots
data = {'A': [1, 2, 2, 3, 4, 5, 100], 'B': [10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10]}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)
plot_ip_boxplots(df, num_cols=1)
# Example with multiple added points and colors
add_points = pd.DataFrame({'A': [1.5, 3.5], 'B': [10, 10]})
plot_ip_boxplots(df, add_points=add_points, add_points_col=["red", "blue"])
```

#### References {.doc-section .doc-section-references}

Bartz-Beielstein, T. (2025). Multi-Objective Optimization and Hyperparameter
Tuning With Desirability Functions. arXiv preprint arXiv:2503.23595.
https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.23595

### plot_ip_histograms { #spotoptim.eda.plots.plot_ip_histograms }

```python
eda.plots.plot_ip_histograms(
    df,
    bins=10,
    num_cols=2,
    figwidth=10,
    thrs_unique=5,
    add_points=None,
    add_points_col=['red'],
)
```

Generate infill-point histograms (ip-histograms) for each numerical column in the DataFrame within a single figure.
The title of each histogram shows the total, unique values count, outliers, and standard deviation.
If there are fewer unique values than the threshold thrs_unique, the ip-histogram is colored differently.
Additional points can be added and highlighted in red.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name           | Type                                           | Description                                                            | Default    |
|----------------|------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| df             | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | DataFrame containing the data to plot.                                 | _required_ |
| bins           | [int](`int`)                                   | Number of bins for the histograms. Defaults to 10.                     | `10`       |
| num_cols       | [int](`int`)                                   | Number of columns in the subplot grid. Defaults to 2.                  | `2`        |
| figwidth       | [int](`int`)                                   | Width of the entire figure. Defaults to 10.                            | `10`       |
| thrs_unique    | [int](`int`)                                   | Threshold for unique values to change histogram color. Defaults to 5.  | `5`        |
| add_points     | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | DataFrame containing additional points to highlight. Defaults to None. | `None`     |
| add_points_col | [list](`list`)                                 | List of colors for the additional points. Defaults to ["red"].         | `['red']`  |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description   |
|--------|--------|---------------|
|        | None   | None          |

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

::: {#9ba0f7c8 .cell execution_count=2}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import pandas as pd
from spotoptim.eda.plots import plot_ip_histograms
data = {'A': [1, 2, 2, 3, 4, 5, 100], 'B': [10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10]}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)
plot_ip_histograms(df, bins=5, num_cols=1, thrs_unique=3)
# Example with multiple added points and colors
add_points = pd.DataFrame({'A': [1.5, 3.5], 'B': [10, 10]})
plot_ip_histograms(df, add_points=add_points, add_points_col=["red", "blue"])
```
